title: Fermented Crispy Croatian Tofu Fritters
description: A cozy, lowcarb Croatian-inspired dish featuring poached tofu in soy milk, mixed with fermented soy sauce and herbs, then fried to a crisp golden finish.

ingredients.name: Firm tofu
ingredients.notes: pressed and cut into 1.5 cm cubes

instructions: Press the tofu for 15 minutes to remove excess moisture, then cut into 1.5 cm cubes.
In a small saucepan, heat the soy milk with a pinch of black pepper until it just reaches a gentle simmer; do not boil.
Add the tofu cubes to the soy milk and poach for 5 minutes, turning gently to coat all sides.
Remove the tofu, drain, and let it sit at room temperature for 5 minutes; then crumble it loosely with a fork.
In a large bowl, combine the crumbled tofu, almond flour, baking powder, smoked paprika, minced garlic, chopped onion, and parsley.
Pour in the fermented soy sauce and stir until the mixture is moist and holds together when pressed between fingers.
Heat the olive oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat; when the oil shimmers, spoon tablespoonfuls of the tofu mixture into the pan.
Flatten each spoonful into a patty with the back of a spoon and cook for 3–4 minutes per side, until golden and crispy.
Transfer cooked fritters to a paper towel‑lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve hot, optionally with a side of tartar sauce made from Greek yogurt and lemon juice for a creamy contrast.
